_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of Cloud-based knowledge management technologies and concepts related to security, governance, procurement, and administration.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of cloud data platform infrastructure services, including managed compute, serverless processing, object storage, data warehouse, and streaming platform options across major cloud providers.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-021 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-015 (AI, cosine: 0.532), TF-K-005 (TF, cosine: 0.527).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.519). Shared vocabulary: [cloud, knowledge]. DCWF-only terms [administration, based, concepts, governance, management, procurement] vs WIDAI-only terms [across, compute, data, infrastructure, major, managed] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.891, R=0.870). P exceeds R by 0.021 — the DCWF element's 15 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.7%). Despite 'Superset of' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of Cloud-based knowledge management technologies and concepts related" and "Knowledge of cloud data platform infrastructure services, including managed comp". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.596 > STS 0.519 (gap +0.076). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [cloud, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.9%. Shared tokens: [cloud, knowledge]. DCWF-only: [administration, based, concepts, governance, management, procurement]. WIDAI-only: [across, compute, data, infrastructure, major, managed].
